short: A learned component that decides which expert or path each input should take.
definition: A routing network is a learned component that decides, for each input, which expert sub-network or computational path to send it through. In a mixture-of-experts model it scores the available experts and picks a small subset per token, so only part of the network runs at a time. Training usually includes a balancing term so work spreads across experts instead of collapsing onto a few.
